exact_answer: Cushing's disease is a pituitary source of excess ACTH, while Cushing's syndrome includes all causes of hypercortisolism including ectopic ACTH and adrenal tumors
Cushing's disease is caused by excessive ACTH secretion by tumors in the pituitary gland, whereas Cushing's syndrome can also result from ectopic ACTH secretion or ACTH-independent adrenal tumors
